clinical_criteria: One or more lower GI scope procedures
ICD9/10 code for diverticulosis or diverticulitis applied within 0-7 days after an abdominal CT scan or lower GI scope
Two or more inpatient admissions associated with an ICD9/10 code for diverticulitis
At least one diverticulitis procedure during the same encounter as an ICD9/10 code for diverticulitis
No more than 1 inpatient admission for diverticulitis
Range of days between diverticular disease ICD9/10 code and CT scan / lower GI scope code from 7 to 30 days (variation)
Requirement of a diverticular disease antibiotic prescription for mild diverticulitis assignment (variation)
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm classifies diverticular disease severity into three categories: 1) Controls (no diverticular disease) - patients with lower GI procedures but no diverticular ICD codes, 2) Diverticulosis - patients with diverticulosis ICD codes but no diverticulitis codes, 3) Mild Diverticulitis - patients with diverticulitis codes and ≤1 inpatient admission, and 4) Operative or Recurrent Inpatient Diverticulitis - patients with either ≥2 inpatient diverticulitis admissions or diverticulitis procedures. The algorithm requires temporal relationships between diagnostic codes and procedures (within 0-7 days of CT scan or colonoscopy). It builds on Pacheco & Thompson's algorithm and incorporates ICD-9, ICD-10, and CPT codes, though the specific codes are referenced in an external data dictionary not provided in these documents.
